title: Not Your Mother's Beans

seo.title: Not Your Mother's Beans - Marinda Kook

html:
Ingredients

  • 45 ml sunflower oil
  • 10 ml black mustard seeds
  • 10 ml onion seeds, optional (they're called 'Nigella')
  • 10 ml coarsely crushed cumin seeds
  • 10 ml finely chopped ginger root
  • 10 ml finely chopped garlic
  • 1 smallish onion, cut into small cubes
  • dried red chilli flakes to taste
  • 750 ml green beans, cut straight into 1 cm pieces
  • 375 ml chopped tomatoes made from peeled and deseeded tomatoes
  • salt and fine white pepper
  • 125 ml roughly chopped coriander leaves

Method

Heat the oil in a wok or deep pan and then add the mustard seeds, onion seeds (if using), and cumin seeds.

As the seeds start to pop, add the ginger, garlic, onion and chilli flakes to taste.

Stir-fry quickly until fragrant and then add the green beans. Stir-fry just until the beans are glossy and lightly cooked.

Now add the chopped tomatoes and stir-fry for a few seconds, just until the tomatoes are heated through – not a moment longer or they'll go mushy and the dish will become watery.

Remove the wok or pan from the heat, season to taste with salt and white pepper, stir in the coriander leaves and serve.

Serves 6 – 8.

Easy!
